markdown:
#  What is the maximum character length for a commitment's 'Number (#)'? 
##  Answer 
When Procore's Company level [ERP Integrations](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore) tool is configured to work with an Integrated ERP System, users can export commitments from Procore to sync them with the ERP system. Depending on the integrated system in your environment, the maximum character limit for the commitment's **Number (#)** field varies.
### Maximum Character Length for Commitment 'Number (#)'
This table details the maximum character limit for the 'Number (#)' field in a Procore Commitment when syncing Procore with an integrated ERP system.  
| VistaÂ®  | 30  | VistaÂ®  | -  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| QuickBooksÂ® Desktop  | 11  | QuickBooksÂ® Desktop  | Longer entries in Procore are truncated during export.  |  
| Sage 100 ContractorÂ®  | 12  | Sage 100 ContractorÂ®  | Longer entries in Procore are truncated during export.  |  
| Sage 300 CREÂ®  | 12  | Sage 300 CREÂ®  | -  |  
| ViewpointÂ® SpectrumÂ®  | 10  | ViewpointÂ® SpectrumÂ®  | -  |  
| Yardi VoyagerÂ®  | 15  | Yardi VoyagerÂ®  | -  |  
| Xeroâ„˘  | 255  | Procore  | Xeroâ„˘ has a 4,000 character limit. However, the maximum allowable number of characters for a commitment in Procore is 255.  |  
#####  Tip
**What is the optimal character length for visibility in Procore?** For maximum visibility, Procore recommends limiting commitment numbers to ten (10) characters. However, the maximum character limit imposed by Procore is 255 characters.
## See Also
  * [What is the maximum character length for a commitment's 'Title'?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-maximum-character-length-for-a-commitment-title)
  * [What do I do when a commitment fails to export from the ERP Integrations tool?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-do-i-do-when-a-commimtment-fails-to-export-from-the-erp-integrations-tool)
  * [Can I customize the numbering system for financial objects in Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-customize-the-numbering-system-for-financial-management-objects-in-procore)


In Procore, the term _integrated ERP System_ , is used to refer to _Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)_ software applications that can be configured to exchange data between Procore and a supported application using the Company level ERP Integrations tool. See [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore)
In Procore, a _Commitment_ is a _purchase order_ or _subcontract_. Both commitment types are contracts that represent a legally enforceable financial agreement between two parties.
title: What is the maximum character length for a commitment's 'Number (#)'?
description: Details the character maximum for a commitment number when using Procore + ERP Integrations.
